arguments.interaction_modes: Voice commands, text chat, gesture recognition.
arguments.design_principles: Minimalist design, high contrast for readability, responsive layout, accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1).
arguments.usability_testing: A/B testing with 50 users, heuristic evaluation, think-aloud protocol.
arguments.output_artifacts: Interactive prototype, persona documentation, usability test report.

tools_called.name: UIUX_Expert

args.user_personas: General consumers aged 18-65, including tech-savvy early adopters, casual users seeking companionship, professionals using the robot for productivity assistance, elderly users needing simplified interactions, and children with parental supervision. Key needs include natural conversation flow, emotional connection, accessibility, and multi-modal interaction capabilities.
args.interaction_modes: Voice commands with natural language processing, text chat via on-screen keyboard, touch-based gesture controls on a companion app, emotion detection through facial expression analysis, haptic feedback for notifications, and optional physical buttons for emergency/quick actions.
args.design_principles: Accessibility-first design (WCAG 2.1 AA compliance), minimalist visual aesthetics with warm and inviting color palettes, progressive disclosure of complex features, micro-interactions for delightful feedback, responsive design across mobile/tablet/desktop, dark mode support, and inclusive design for users with varying physical and cognitive abilities.
args.prototyping_tools: Figma for high-fidelity UI mockups and interactive prototypes, Adobe XD for animation prototyping, Principle for micro-interaction design, and Maze for remote usability testing integration.
args.usability_testing: A/B testing for conversation flow variants, heuristic evaluation by UX experts, moderated remote usability sessions with 20 participants across target personas, eye-tracking studies for visual attention analysis, SUS (System Usability Scale) scoring, and longitudinal diary studies over 4 weeks to assess long-term engagement and emotional connection.
args.output_artifacts: Interactive Figma prototype with full conversation flows, UI component library and design system documentation, user journey maps for all personas, usability test report with actionable recommendations, accessibility audit report, and final design specification handoff document for development.

args.system_design: A multi-modal chatting robot interaction system featuring: (1) a frontend client layer with voice/text/gesture input processing, (2) a real-time communication gateway using WebSocket for low-latency bidirectional messaging, (3) an AI orchestration layer for NLP, emotion recognition, and context management, (4) a user profile and memory service for personalized conversations, (5) a haptic and notification service for multi-sensory feedback, and (6) a companion mobile/web app with offline fallback capabilities. The system must support concurrent users with sub-200ms response latency for voice interactions.
args.technology_stack: Frontend: React Native (mobile), React (web), SwiftUI (iOS native widgets); Backend: Node.js with Express, Python FastAPI for AI microservices; Real-time: Socket.io / WebSocket; AI/ML: OpenAI GPT API, TensorFlow Lite for on-device emotion detection, Whisper for speech-to-text, ElevenLabs for text-to-speech; Database: PostgreSQL (user data), Redis (session cache), MongoDB (conversation history); Cloud: AWS (ECS, S3, CloudFront); CI/CD: GitHub Actions, Docker, Kubernetes.
args.hardware_requirements: Robot side: Quad-core ARM Cortex-A72 CPU, 8GB RAM, far-field microphone array (4+ mics), HD camera with IR for facial recognition, haptic actuator, 7-inch touch display (1024x600), Wi-Fi 6 / Bluetooth 5.2 module, 64GB eMMC storage; Server side: Auto-scaling cloud instances (min 2 vCPU, 8GB RAM per instance), GPU instances for AI inference (NVIDIA T4), CDN for static assets, load balancer with SSL termination.
args.interaction_interface: Primary: Voice-first conversational UI with wake-word detection ("Hey Robot"), real-time speech waveform visualization, and emotion-aware response tone adaptation. Secondary: Companion mobile app with chat bubbles, quick-action cards, mood tracking dashboard, and settings panel. Tertiary: On-robot touch screen with minimalist card-based UI, large touch targets (min 48x48dp), high-contrast mode, and gesture navigation (swipe, tap, long-press). Emergency: Physical mute button and SOS trigger on the robot body.
args.test_optimization_plan: Unit testing: Jest (frontend), PyTest (backend); Integration testing: Cypress for E2E flows; Performance testing: k6 for WebSocket load testing (target: 10k concurrent connections), Lighthouse for web vitals; Voice accuracy: WER (Word Error Rate) benchmarking under various noise conditions; A/B testing: LaunchDarkly feature flags for gradual rollout; Monitoring: Datadog for real-time observability, Sentry for error tracking, PagerDuty for on-call alerts; Continuous optimization: weekly model fine-tuning based on user feedback loops.

test_cases.input: Please provide a website development proposal, including website architecture, database design, and user experience optimization.

arguments.system_design: A three-tier website architecture with a presentation layer (React.js frontend), an application layer (Node.js/Express backend), and a data layer (PostgreSQL). Incorporates CDN and load balancer for scalability and performance, along with secure API endpoints and modular components for maintainability.
arguments.technology_stack: Frontend: React.js, Backend: Node.js with Express, Database: PostgreSQL, Hosting: AWS EC2 & S3, CDN: CloudFront, Version Control: GitHub
arguments.hardware_requirements: Cloud-based hosting with minimum 2 vCPUs, 8GB RAM for app servers, AWS RDS for database, and 50GB SSD storage; scalable load balancer configuration for peak loads.
arguments.interaction_interface: Responsive web interface with mobile-first design, intuitive navigation, accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1), and optimized load times; includes interactive UI elements and seamless database-driven content updates.
arguments.test_optimization_plan: Automated unit and integration testing using Jest and Cypress, load testing with Apache JMeter, continuous performance monitoring via New Relic, and iterative A/B testing for UI/UX refinement.

tools_called.name: system_architecture_designer

args.system_design: A modern full-stack web application with a microservices-inspired modular architecture, including a React/Next.js frontend, Node.js/Express API gateway, dedicated backend services, PostgreSQL relational database layer, Redis caching layer, and CloudFront CDN for static asset delivery. The system supports horizontal scaling via container orchestration.
args.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS; Backend: Node.js, Express, Python (for data services); Database: PostgreSQL 16, Redis; Infrastructure: Docker, Kubernetes (EKS), AWS (EC2, S3, CloudFront, RDS); Monitoring: Datadog, Sentry
